sentences.text: Hi, Lauren!
Hello, how are y'all?
I'm doing well, how are you?
Doing well!
Good!
I was looking back on our conversations, I was like, me and Lauren just chatted yesterday, and then I.
No, that was…
April, and then I was like, oh my gosh, and the time before that was, like, this time last year.
How have you been?
I've been well, time is just, as you're alluding to, absolutely flying by.
it feels faster lately, right?
Like, this year, I feel like I can't keep my head on straight.
Like, it's just so much.
And you're so sweet for hopping on.
I just listened… I just listened to Jack's calls.
And I was like, okay, so I… I do want to be respectful of your time, because I know that, of course, we've met.

And I know last time that we spoke, there was a student property that we were chatting about, and it had multiple buildings, and we were talking about just not being able to support that many floor plans.
And I heard that y'all just rolled out a tool in July, is that correct?
For… for, like, our Google listings and stuff.
Yes, yeah.
Okay, so we didn't roll one out in July, coming up next July, our current contract with what we're using right now will expire.
Got it.
Okay, which… I try to, like, get as much information from our other calls, just, of course, to be mindful of your time.
And I know that, of course, like, initiatives change and whatnot, but y'all typically do budget… like, in the spring, is that right?
Your budgeting season's kind of…
No, it's actually about to launch, like, Any day now.
Okay, okay, perfect.
So it might make sense, then, for us to kind of chit-chat for a second?
Yeah, sure.
Okay, okay, perfect.
Cause yeah, we do have some updates since we chatted last.

There, yeah, there's a lot.

17-ish.
Okay, okay.
Okay, perfect.

Let me see here…
I say I've added a couple, they're not optimized at all.

We're at about 30.

So those… that's 30 profiles, right?
Yes.

Where the students are, you know, used more frequently.

residents and… and whatnot.

for.

one, that smile, okay.

Who your property management software is?

Appfolio.

this year, how much of an initiative it is for property management companies.
So, as you know, because, of course, y'all focus on those Google Ads.
Google still dominates search.

You know, with 87% of runners starting their search on Google, we of course want to make sure that your profiles look as great as possible, and that also prospective residents are able to gather as much information on your profile just at a glance.
So, beyond that digital curb appeal, what Google rewards is relevant, frequent, and unique content to the profiles.
So, everything that we do, again, is focused on optimizing that organic SEO.
We have plenty of partners who will scale back their Google Ads spend.
you know, not entirely, I'm not saying, like, this is going to, like.
you know, completely replace it.
However, that is something that we see as far as a trend of rolling this out, and then over time, being able to scale back that, PPC spent.
So… As you kind of alluded to, like, y'all are tasked with so much.
It's, never seems like y'all have enough resources to cover everything and manage everything.
On-site teams are overburdened, and ultimately the renter experience is not standardized.
So, as a refresher, we integrate with Appfolio to do 4 things on the profile daily.
We're gonna pull over your, available floor plans with the starting price, we'll leverage AI to create posts using your photos.
We manage your Q&A feed, and then we also get concessions and specials on there when they are available.
So, I wanted to pull up, one that, I mean.
Is Champaign close to North Aurora, or did I totally miss the mark with this one?
No, you're good.
It's probably, like, 2 hours north of the.
That's so funny.
Chicago.
So, not clothes.
Okay.
I'm very used to it, anytime, you know, someone says Illinois, it's automatically assumed Chicago, right?
So…

Well, no, no, I always try to find, like, something nearby, but anyway, Apologies, this is not closer to Champaign, but yes, did want to pull up a profile, so they, of course, use our Google Business Profile automation tool, and again, like, this the AI just continues to evolve, which is so freaky and cool, I guess, depending on… how we're using it, but in this regard, it has been really neat to see the language of the AI model continue to evolve.
So, again, what we do here is we try to leverage as many amenity-rich keywords on the post.

We can incorporate any of those PPC keywords that y'all are focused on as well to, again, we just want to make sure that As someone is searching for apartments near me with a fitness center, or a patio, what have you, you will come up in that feed.
Okay.
So there is that, and then, wanted to show this products feed.

But we now can additionally include both in-unit and community amenities, because again.
The more that we can put those keywords on the profile, the more likely you are to show up in those searches.
Nice.
So there is that, and then, the Q&A feed, that continues to evolve as well, with, you know, amenities kind of evolving as well.
I know that EVs have been around for a while, but EV chargers continue to show up on more and more properties.
So, we continue to reflect.
those questions appropriately.

So, yes, as a reminder, what we've done here is we looked at the most frequently asked questions to the Q&A feed across multifamily, and we saw that a lot of the times, the questions would not be responded to, or they would be responded to, you know, like, months or years later.

So what we've done to even, you know, prevent a prospective resident needing to ask the question is we place those questions on the profile, and then we edit the answer daily, even if it doesn't change, because we, again, want to make sure that Google is rewarding y'all with that frequent, updating.
Nice.
On that note, I did want to also show you, this is gonna be a new update, so… we can help with Facebook and Instagram automated posts, now.

let's say we do not like this post at all, you can just completely regenerate the post.
So…
Thanks.

So, just really quickly, we can actually pretend that, like, we want this to go on all three channels.

Nice.

Beyond the website onto Google, which is really, you know, our focus.
So… so, yes, this has also evolved a little bit with being able to connect to external websites that will then crawl that website and learn your language, to better reflect that in the posts.
Nice, okay.

And I know the student properties, with them having, you know, those scattered sites, maybe not the best fit, with being able to, support all of those floor plans.

So I haven't heard a ton directly, like.
In that format.

So I would say that is probably the biggest focus, and then also…
Okay.
like.

transparently, Google Business Profiles have kind of been, you know, really huge for hotels, for restaurants, and multifamily was kind of maybe, like, a little bit late to the game, and that kind of being on their radars.

Okay.
just Google rewarding those profiles with that organic SEO a little bit more than historically they have been.

Yeah.

you know, you're not… you can't advertise on ChatGPT, so is that SEO gonna become even more important?.

Okay.
But, I mean, full transparency, like, yeah, as ChatGPT evolves, I'm looking at this tool, and I'm like, this is great.
